diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Perioral paraesthesia is an early, characteristic feature of a falling ionised calcium.
Respiratory alkalosis from hyperventilation can produce the same perioral tingling — non-discriminating on its own.

symptoms.symptom_name: Carpopedal spasm / tetany (hands locked in rigid flexion)
symptoms.character: painful, sustained flexor spasm the patient cannot voluntarily release

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Overt carpopedal tetany indicates severe hypocalcaemia and heralds laryngospasm/seizure.
Alkalosis can induce carpopedal spasm too, but here it compounds an organic hypocalcaemia rather than being the sole cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Four days of calcium and calcitriol non-adherence

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: In a PTH-resistant patient, withdrawing calcium and active vitamin D is the direct precipitant of falling serum calcium.
A lifelong calcium-dependent disorder makes hypocalcaemia the front-running explanation for new tetany.

symptoms.symptom_name: Gritty subcutaneous feeling over the joints (subcutaneous calcification)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Subcutaneous calcification (osteoma cutis) is part of the Albright hereditary osteodystrophy phenotype.
Panic-driven hyperventilation causes respiratory alkalosis that acutely lowers ionised calcium and worsens tetany.
A confounder — it exacerbates but does not cause the underlying hypocalcaemia.

symptoms.symptom_name: Chvostek's sign (facial twitch on tapping the facial nerve)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Neuromuscular hyperexcitability of hypocalcaemia — clinician-elicited, not available over telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Trousseau's sign (carpal spasm on BP-cuff inflation)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A positive Trousseau's sign is a specific bedside indicator of hypocalcaemic tetany — clinician-elicited only.

symptoms.symptom_name: Shortened 4th/5th metacarpals with knuckle dimpling (Archibald's sign)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Brachydactyly of the 4th/5th metacarpals is a hallmark of the Albright hereditary osteodystrophy phenotype.

symptoms.symptom_name: Firm subcutaneous calcifications over the extensor forearms

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Palpable osteoma cutis supports the AHO phenotype — requires clinician palpation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal routine vital signs (BP 115/75, HR 98, RR 20, SpO2 99%, afebrile)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reassuring routine observations do NOT exclude a life-threatening hypocalcaemic emergency — the reassuring-vitals trap.

investigation_details.expected_result: Low — anticipated on hospital biochemistry, pending
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The severity of hypocalcaemia and response to repletion cannot be quantified; delays definitive IV calcium.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A low measured calcium confirms the diagnosis; unavailable via telehealth in real time.

investigation_details.expected_result: Elevated despite low calcium — indicates PTH resistance (pseudohypoparathyroidism), pending hospital labs
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Cannot distinguish pseudo- from primary hypoparathyroidism, though acute management is unchanged.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Elevated PTH with low calcium distinguishes pseudohypoparathyroidism from primary hypoparathyroidism.

investigation_details.expected_result: QT prolongation possible with severe hypocalcaemia; requires hospital monitoring

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: QT prolongation from hypocalcaemia carries arrhythmia risk — mandates cardiac monitoring; not obtainable via telehealth.
Excludes an acute GI-loss contributor and points back to medication non-adherence as the precipitant.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Perioral paraesthesia + carpopedal spasm + positive Chvostek/Trousseau is the classic emergency constellation of symptomatic hypocalcaemia.
Short 4th/5th metacarpals + subcutaneous calcification + the known short-stature/round-facies phenotype localise the underlying disorder to pseudohypoparathyroidism.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms and quantifies hypocalcaemia and identifies co-existing magnesium derangement; done in hospital after emergency transfer.
Severe hypocalcaemia prolongs the QT and risks arrhythmia during repletion.
Characterises the underlying disorder (elevated PTH = resistance) once the acute emergency is controlled.
